attack_surface.data_description: Forged claim request using compromised credentials of a legitimate Airdrop Recipient.

security_gap.gap_description: Authentication of the Airdrop Recipient (Actor 8) to the Airdrop Claim Tool (Component 222) is compromised via stolen private keys.

actor_risk.description: Airdrop Recipient's private keys are compromised, allowing an attacker to impersonate them and steal their airdrop.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise of user's private keys allows an attacker to perform any action on their behalf, including claiming airdrops.

assumptions: The Airdrop Recipient (Actor 8) has had their private keys stolen or exposed through malware, phishing, or other means.
The Airdrop Claim Tool relies solely on cryptographic signatures for authentication, which the attacker can now produce.

mitigation.strategy: Users must secure their private keys. Encourage use of hardware wallets and best security practices. The airdrop mechanism itself cannot prevent this if keys are compromised, but time-limited claim windows can reduce the window of opportunity.

description: An attacker, having compromised the private keys of a legitimate Airdrop Recipient (Actor 8), spoofs their identity to the Airdrop Claim Tool (Component 222) and successfully claims the $BERA airdrop on behalf of the victim, directing it to an attacker-controlled address.
